q: According to Newton's second law, an object that is ___ must have a net force acting on it.
An object moving in a circle must have a force applied to it to keep it moving in the ___.
The force applied to keep an object moving in a circle is known as the ___ force.
For uniform circular motion, the speed $v = ___$.
The acceleration in uniform circular motion is ___ acceleration.
Centripetal acceleration is directed towards the ___ of the circle at any moment.
According to Newton's second law, an object in a circular motion requires a ___ force directed towards the centre of the circle.
In the absence of centripetal force, the object would move in a ___ line.
Newton's ___ law states that in the absence of a net force, an object would move in a straight line.
The magnitude of the centripetal force is given by $F_c = m a_c = ___$.
Substituting $v = r \omega$ into the centripetal force equation gives $F_c = ___$.
The gravitational force of the earth on the ___ orbiting the earth is an example of centripetal force.
The gravitational force of the ___ on the planets orbiting it provides centripetal force.
The ___ force acting on an electron orbiting the nucleus provides the centripetal force.
When whirling a body attached to a string, a ___ force is originated.
If the tension force is ___ to the direction of motion, it causes the body to move in a circular path.
An ___ force exists between the sun and the earth in a direction perpendicular to the path of the earth.
The ___ attractive force between the nucleus and the electron acts as the required centripetal force.
When a car turns in a circular path, a ___ force exists between the road and the car tires.
Frictional force acts normally to the direction of motion of the car, causing the car to move in a ___ path.
When a car moves in a circular path which is inclined to the horizontal, the ___ force of the road acts normally to the car.
The reaction force of the road is also known as the ___ force.
Resolving the normal force into two components, the ___ component acts towards the centre of the circle.
The horizontal component of normal force is the ___ centripetal force.
The upward thrust acts normally to an ___ body.
Upward thrust is also referred to as ___ force.
When the aeroplane tilts, the horizontal component acts normally to the motion of the aeroplane, causing it to turn in a ___.
The horizontal component of the ___ force on the aeroplane provides the centripetal force.
On a flat road, also called an ___ road, centripetal force is supplied by friction between the tires and the road.
The frictional force $F_f$ acting upon the ___ of the car provides the required centripetal force.
The formula for frictional force providing centripetal force is $F_f = ___$.
In the formula for centripetal force, $r$ is the ___ of the path.
If the frictional force is not ___, the car will skid out of the circular path.
Wet and ___ conditions can make frictional force insufficient for a car to turn safely.
Banking of roads is raising the ___ edge of the curved road higher than the inner edge.
The angle that the inclined track makes with horizontal is called ___ of banking.
The normal force $F_N$ exerted by a banked road is ___ to the road.
For every banked curve, there is a ___ speed at which the entire centripetal force is supplied by the horizontal component of the normal force.
In the case of limiting speed, frictional force between the tires and the road is not ___.
The horizontal component of the normal force is given by $F_N \sin \theta = ___$.
Since there is no ___ motion, the net force on car in y direction is zero.
The vertical component of normal force is $F_N \cos \theta = ___$.
Dividing the horizontal component by the vertical component of force yields $\tan \theta = ___$.
The banking angle $\theta$ is chosen so that the condition for ___ speed $v$ holds.
A force is needed to ___ from its straight line path to move in a circle.
The gravitational force of the sun on the ___ provides centripetal force.
Tension in the string provides the required centripetal force for a ___ to move in circular path.
The horizontal component of normal force acts towards the ___ of the curve.
The net force on a car in the $y$ direction is ___ when there is no vertical motion.
The ___ of banking $\theta$ is chosen based on the limiting speed $v$ and radius $r$.
Centripetal force is the force acting ___ the centre of the circular path.
___ force appears to act in a direction pointing away from the centre.
A person whirling a ball on a string in a horizontal circle exerts an ___ force on the ball.
According to Newton's ___ law, the ball being whirled exerts a reaction force on the hand.
There is a ___ that the reaction force on the hand is the outward-directed centrifugal force acting on the ball.
Except centripetal force, there is no other force acting on the ball along the ___.
When a car moves around a curve, the person feels an ___ force exerted on him.
The outward force felt in a turning car is the centrifugal force caused by the effect of ___.
Actually, the centrifugal force is not a ___ force.
Centrifugal force is an apparent or ___ force.
Centrifugal force is equal and ___ to the centripetal force.
The concept of centrifugal force can be applied in ___ devices.
A ___ is a piece of equipment that puts an object in rotation around a fixed axis.
In a laboratory centrifuge, the ___ acceleration causes denser particles to settle to the bottom.
___ particles settle to the bottom of the tube in a centrifuge.
Low density substances ___ to the top in a centrifuge.
The effect of particles settling or rising in a centrifuge is known as ___ principle.
A centrifugal pump is a ___ machine which converts mechanical energy into hydraulic energy.
Hydraulic energy is also known as ___ energy.
The rotating ___ moves water or other fluids by using centrifugal force in a pump.
The centrifugal action of a ___ machine is used in spin drying the clothes.
When clothes are spun very fast, excess water is removed completely with the help of ___ force.
The ___ governor is a device used to measure and regulate the speed of a machine.
Centrifugal governors consist of two balls of ___ mass.
In a centrifugal governor, balls revolve around the ___ at a certain speed.
Centripetal force is directed towards the ___ of rotation or centre of curvature.
Centrifugal force acts ___ away from the centre of the circle.
A ___ orbiting a planet is supported by the centripetal force.
Removing excess water by ___ drying clothes is with the help of centrifugal force.
Centrifugal force results from the effect of ___.
Road banking is an ___ of centripetal forces.
Rotating devices like centrifuges are applications of ___ forces.
The inward force exerted by a person pulling a string is the required ___ force.
A person on a moving car feels no force when moving with constant velocity along a ___ road.
Centrifugal governors are used to regulate the speed of an ___.
The balls of a centrifugal governor are attached to the ___.
Centrifugal force is equal and opposite to the ___ force.
In a centrifuge, radial acceleration causes particles to move according to their ___.
Centrifugal pumps convert mechanical energy into ___ energy.
Passengers in a car experience an ___ push due to the centrifugal force when taking a sudden turn.
The centripetal force $F_c = m a_c$ is directed towards the ___ of curvature.
Centrifugal force appears to act in a direction ___ from the centre.
The centrifugal governor maintains a certain ___ due to centrifugal force to regulate speed.
Excess water is removed from clothes by the centrifugal action of a ___ machine.
Centrifugal pumps move water or other ___ using centrifugal force.
In circular motion, two different forces encountered are ___ and centrifugal forces.
The outward push in a car takes place when it moves in a ___ path.
A centrifuge spins an object in a circle around a ___ axis.
Spin drying is an application of ___ force.
The centripetal force is the force acting on an object in ___ motion.
